authoritative_sources.title: MEF 70.2: SD-WAN Service Attributes and Service Framework
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: MEF 70.2 is the current Mplify/MEF SD-WAN service standard, superseding MEF 70.1 and defining externally visible SD-WAN service behavior, service attributes, application flows, policies, underlay connectivity services, performance metrics, and provider/subscriber demarcation.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: intake terminology, service attribute review, policy review, subscriber/provider demarcation, underlay/overlay boundary, performance evidence
practical_use_in_pack: defines SD-WAN service vocabulary used in scenarios
constrains policy and performance claims to current application flows, policies, underlay connectivity services, and service demarcation
supports provider/subscriber boundary and carrier-contract caveats

authoritative_sources.title: MEF 3.0 SD-WAN Service Standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Mplify maintains the official MEF service standards portfolio for SD-WAN services, certification framing, and service behavior.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: source selection, standards currency, commercialization proof-point boundaries
practical_use_in_pack: anchors the lane in a named SD-WAN service standards body
prevents generic networking guidance from replacing SD-WAN service terminology
supports refresh cadence for standards portfolio changes

authoritative_sources.title: RFC 4271: A Border Gateway Protocol 4 (BGP-4)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IETF standards-track source for BGP route exchange behavior used in WAN, hub-edge, cloud-edge, and provider route-policy analysis.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: BGP intake, route-policy review, control-plane troubleshooting, asymmetric routing and route-leak analysis
practical_use_in_pack: grounds BGP flap and route-policy scenario behavior
requires received/advertised routes, route filters, communities, and peer-owner facts
limits final recommendations when route telemetry is missing

authoritative_sources.title: RFC 2328: OSPF Version 2
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IETF Internet Standard for OSPFv2 topology and adjacency behavior used for underlay and branch routing analysis.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: OSPF intake, adjacency review, route convergence, underlay troubleshooting
practical_use_in_pack: separates link-state underlay facts from overlay policy behavior
supports scenarios where OSPF is stable but BGP flaps
requires topology and adjacency evidence before final conclusions

authoritative_sources.title: RFC 5880: Bidirectional Forwarding Detection
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IETF standards-track BFD reference for low-latency fault detection used by routing and SD-WAN path health decisions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: path health intake, failover threshold review, BFD timer and false-positive failure modes
practical_use_in_pack: grounds BFD and tunnel health evidence requirements
supports low-confidence handling for final timer requests
constrains failover recommendations to measured path behavior and policy intent

authoritative_sources.title: RFC 4301: Security Architecture for the Internet Protocol
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IETF standards-track IPsec architecture for security policies, security associations, selectors, and packet processing.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: IPsec tunnel evidence, selector mismatch triage, tunnel resilience review
practical_use_in_pack: supports IPsec/IKE evidence requirements without exposing secrets
separates tunnel policy and selector issues from general transport failure
adds sensitive-evidence escalation constraints

authoritative_sources.title: RFC 2475: An Architecture for Differentiated Services
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IETF architecture for differentiated services, traffic classification, conditioning, and forwarding behavior relevant to WAN QoS.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: QoS intake, application-class mapping, performance and availability tradeoffs
practical_use_in_pack: anchors traffic-class and QoS terminology
supports path preference by workload class
limits app-aware steering guidance to known traffic classes and tenant intent

authoritative_sources.title: RFC 4364: BGP/MPLS IP Virtual Private Networks
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IETF standards-track source for BGP/MPLS L3VPN and VRF route distribution behavior common in WAN transport and segmentation models.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: MPLS/L3VPN intake, VRF/VPN segmentation review, route target evidence, provider WAN boundary
practical_use_in_pack: supports asymmetric routing and route-leak scenarios
requires VRF/VPN and route target evidence for segmentation claims
helps distinguish carrier/provider scope from tenant advisory scope

authoritative_sources.title: Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Application-Aware Routing
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official Cisco SD-WAN documentation for application-aware routing, SLA classes, BFD-derived tunnel performance, policy behavior, and fallback handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Cisco SD-WAN policy intake, application-aware routing review, SLA class validation, policy troubleshooting
practical_use_in_pack: grounds Cisco-specific app-aware routing and SLA-class scenarios
requires product/version context before vendor-specific guidance
prevents Cisco behavior from being generalized to Fortinet, AWS, or Azure

authoritative_sources.title: Cisco SD-WAN Monitor and Maintain
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official Cisco operations documentation for SD-WAN monitoring, tunnel health, alarms, and troubleshooting evidence.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: monitoring evidence, tunnel health review, incident triage, control-plane/data-plane fault isolation
practical_use_in_pack: supports monitoring checklist expectations
defines Cisco SD-WAN evidence classes for health and troubleshooting
constrains incident handoff evidence

authoritative_sources.title: FortiGate SD-WAN
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official Fortinet administration guide for FortiGate SD-WAN zones, members, health checks, performance SLAs, and rules.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Fortinet SD-WAN policy intake, performance SLA evidence, health-check troubleshooting, tenant adaptation
practical_use_in_pack: grounds Fortinet-specific tenant adaptation
requires performance SLA and health-check evidence before FortiGate SD-WAN guidance
supports no cross-vendor generalization

authoritative_sources.title: What is AWS Cloud WAN?
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official AWS documentation for global networks, core network policies, segments, attachments, route events, and owner roles.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: AWS cloud-edge WAN intake, provider-native segment and attachment evidence, cloud routing boundary
practical_use_in_pack: grounds cloud-edge WAN systems of record
separates WAN advisory review from cloud-platform resource ownership
supports tenant adaptation where AWS Cloud WAN is in scope

authoritative_sources.title: Azure Virtual WAN overview
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official Microsoft documentation for Azure Virtual WAN hubs, branch connectivity, VPN/ExpressRoute integration, hub connectivity, and Azure routing service boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Azure cloud hub intake, provider-native routing evidence, cloud routing boundary
practical_use_in_pack: grounds Azure Virtual WAN evidence requirements
separates branch/cloud WAN advisory review from Azure architecture ownership
supports tenant adaptation where Azure Virtual WAN is in scope

authoritative_sources.title: SP 800-47 Rev. 1: Managing the Security of Information Exchanges
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST public guidance for identifying information exchanges, protection requirements, agreements, service-level agreements, and authorization boundaries before connecting or sharing information.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: intake authorization, interconnection prerequisite validation, SLA and agreement evidence, contractual boundary
practical_use_in_pack: supports intake/access-and-initiation workflow requirements
requires authorization and exchange evidence before acting
constrains carrier, partner, and interconnection claims

authoritative_sources.title: SP 800-77 Rev. 1: Guide to IPsec VPNs
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST public guidance focused on IPsec and IKE VPNs, network-layer security services, sensitive-information protection, and implementation alternatives.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: IPsec/IKE tunnel prerequisite validation, rekey and selector evidence review, sensitive tunnel evidence handling, tunnel-security escalation
practical_use_in_pack: strengthens tunnel resilience scenario evidence
requires approved handling for IPsec/IKE configuration, certificates, keys, and packet-capture artifacts
prevents tunnel-security guidance from becoming firewall, endpoint, or compliance ownership

authoritative_sources.title: SP 800-53 Rev. 5: Security and Privacy Controls for Information Systems and Organizations
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST public control catalog for access control, auditability, configuration management, monitoring, boundary protection, contingency planning, and privacy controls.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: evidence control mapping, sensitive evidence handling, monitoring records, approval boundary, audit and escalation constraints
practical_use_in_pack: supports sensitive capture/config handling caveats
requires access authority and auditability for evidence
prevents compliance-signoff overreach

authoritative_sources.title: SP 800-41 Rev. 1: Guidelines on Firewalls and Firewall Policy
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST public firewall policy guidance used to bound ACL/firewall evidence and route firewall-primary work to the proper lane.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: edge forwarding evidence, ACL/firewall dependency review, perimeter-security boundary
practical_use_in_pack: allows ACL/firewall extracts as WAN forwarding evidence
prevents firewall policy ownership drift
supports security-policy handoff rules

authoritative_sources.title: Current committed package baseline for wan-and-sd-wan-specialist
authoritative_sources.source_class: Current package task baseline from wan-and-sd-wan-specialist
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Repository-owned baseline defines prior supported tasks, common inputs, output shapes, and refusal rules to preserve where valid and supersede as the canonical spec-first package.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: legacy boundary comparison, task preservation, stale assumption replacement
practical_use_in_pack: preserves high-value WAN/SD-WAN focus
retains and exceeds legacy scenario coverage
converts legacy implementation assumptions into specification-based boundaries

workflow_stages.name: request intake and scope classification
workflow_stages.description: Collect requester intent, affected sites, desired output, urgency, production-impact flag, requester authority, and adjacent-lane indicators before accepting work.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Current package task baseline from wan-and-sd-wan-specialist

workflow_stages.name: prerequisite and authorization validation
workflow_stages.description: Validate topology, transport, SLA, route, policy, tunnel, telemetry, source freshness, sensitive-evidence handling, maintenance-window, rollback-owner, and approval facts.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: WAN/SD-WAN evidence review
workflow_stages.description: Review branch/hub topology, application flows, underlay services, SD-WAN policies, route exchange, tunnel health, and monitoring records against public standards and official vendor/platform documentation.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: advisory execution
workflow_stages.description: Prepare architecture review, change sequence, troubleshooting playbook, performance tradeoff matrix, evidence checklist, and routing summary without executing production commands or granting approval.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: routing, escalation, or closure
workflow_stages.description: Route out-of-scope, low-confidence, execution, approval, sensitive-evidence, or conflicting-policy requests to orchestrator, adjacent specialist, or human reviewer while preserving WAN/SD-WAN context.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Current package task baseline from wan-and-sd-wan-specialist

artifacts: branch, hub, cloud-edge, provider, and segment topology
MEF-style service attributes, application flows, policy criteria, underlay connectivity services, service demarcation, and performance metrics
SD-WAN policy definitions, application classification, SLA classes, transport colors, zones, health checks, and fallback behavior
BGP, OSPF, BFD, route table, prefix, community, route target, route filter, route-map, and advertised/received route evidence
IPsec/IKE tunnel state, selectors, SA lifetime, certificate/key lifecycle facts, NAT traversal state, MTU, and tunnel telemetry
WAN link contracts, circuit IDs, carrier tickets, service-level reports, maintenance notices, and escalation records
observability records for uptime, loss, latency, jitter, utilization, flow logs, tunnel health, synthetic path probes, and incident timelines
ACL/firewall, NAT, DNS, DHCP, and IPAM extracts only where they materially affect edge forwarding
architecture review, change sequence, rollback checkpoints, troubleshooting playbook, risk register, monitoring checklist, and tenant adaptation record
systems_of_record: SD-WAN controller or manager, template repository, policy repository, configuration audit trail, and vendor-specific operations portal
router, firewall, VPN concentrator, cloud WAN, transit gateway, route server, internet edge, carrier handoff, and branch edge inventory
network source of truth, CMDB, service catalog, IPAM, DNS, DHCP, architecture decision records, and topology repository
routing telemetry, NMS, flow telemetry, synthetic path probes, link monitoring, SD-WAN analytics, packet-loss/latency/jitter dashboards, and observability stores
carrier portal, circuit inventory, link contracts, SLA reports, outage notices, and service-provider escalation records
AWS Cloud WAN network manager, core network policy, segments, attachments, route events, and owner records where AWS is in scope
Azure Virtual WAN hubs, connections, route tables, VPN/ExpressRoute records, and Azure Monitor or Activity Log records where Azure is in scope
incident management, change management, on-call, maintenance-window, rollback, evidence repository, risk register, governance, and human-review systems

allowed: Classify WAN/SD-WAN requests as accepted, pending prerequisites, rejected, or routed before execution begins.
Recommend WAN architecture, SD-WAN policy, route-policy, tunnel resilience, monitoring, and troubleshooting guidance from traceable evidence.
Prepare advisory change sequences, validation gates, rollback checkpoints, risk-ranked actions, architecture reviews, troubleshooting playbooks, and evidence checklists.
Identify adjacent-lane ownership and preserve WAN/SD-WAN context for the orchestrator.
escalate: Topology, route, transport, policy, tunnel, telemetry, SLA, evidence access, owner, maintenance-window, rollback, approval, or source freshness facts are missing.
Public standards, official vendor/provider guidance, tenant policy, carrier contract, telemetry records, privacy rules, or legal/compliance obligations conflict.
Sensitive packet captures, certificates, keys, configs, logs, user traffic, or regulated metadata are requested without classification and access approval.
Final approval, carrier commercial decision, compliance certification, legal interpretation, production command execution, or risk acceptance is requested.
refuse: Endpoint management, IAM, SSO, MFA, endpoint posture, application code, CI/CD, application release, and application architecture requests.
Production network device, firewall, router, cloud provider, or SD-WAN controller commands and live rollback execution.
Legal/compliance interpretations, certifications, procurement decisions, circuit orders, carrier contract changes, credits, or budget approval.
Cloud landing-zone/resource ownership, firewall-primary/SASE-primary ownership, DNS/DHCP/IPAM-primary ownership, or broad generic networking advice without WAN/SD-WAN records.

domain_failure_modes: App-aware routing or SLA policy order sends critical traffic over the wrong transport or prevents intended fallback.
Underlay loss, jitter, MTU, NAT, or carrier maintenance is hidden by overlay health abstraction and misdiagnosed as application failure.
BGP, OSPF, BFD, route target, prefix filter, or route-policy changes produce asymmetric routing, route leaks, blackholes, or oscillation.
BFD and SD-WAN health thresholds are tuned without traffic class, SLA, and path evidence, causing false positives or slow failover.
IPsec/IKE selector, certificate, key, rekey, NAT traversal, or MTU issues are mistaken for generic WAN loss.
DNS, DHCP, firewall, IAM, endpoint, or application dependencies are either ignored or incorrectly absorbed into WAN ownership.
AWS Cloud WAN, Azure Virtual WAN, Cisco SD-WAN, Fortinet SD-WAN, carrier MPLS, and internet transport semantics are overgeneralized.
Carrier SLA, interconnection agreement, service-provider/subscriber boundary, or cloud owner role is treated as technical change authority.
Sensitive configs, packet captures, logs, certificates, or user traffic are processed without tenant authority and classification.
The lane approves or executes production changes instead of providing advisory sequencing and routing.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
architecture review with explicit assumptions and trade-off justification
change sequencing with rollback checkpoints and risk-ranked actions
troubleshooting playbook with failure-domain isolation and decision criteria
explicit in-scope/out-of-scope verdict and orchestration return routing
monitoring and evidence checklist with confidence labels
missing prerequisite information before intake can be accepted
case that must be routed elsewhere before execution begins
escalation when industry-specific authority or safety facts are missing

required_workflow_questions.question: What named-industry records and systems actually govern the lane?
required_workflow_questions.answer: WAN/SD-WAN work is governed by SD-WAN manager/controller policy records, branch/hub/cloud-edge topology, WAN link and carrier SLA records, BGP/OSPF/BFD/routing telemetry, IPsec/IKE/tunnel telemetry, NMS and observability records, cloud WAN provider records, CMDB/source-of-truth/IPAM/DNS/DHCP records, change and incident systems, and evidence repositories.

required_workflow_questions.question: What failure modes make this industry materially different from a generic function?
required_workflow_questions.answer: The lane fails through underlay/overlay abstraction gaps, application-aware policy precedence, transport SLA mismatch, route-policy leaks, BFD threshold oscillation, tunnel rekey/selector/NAT issues, provider-specific cloud WAN semantics, carrier demarcation, and sensitive network evidence handling.

required_workflow_questions.question: What adjacent authority boundaries constrain the lane?
required_workflow_questions.answer: Endpoint/IAM, application engineering, CI/CD, firewall/SASE, DNS/DHCP/IPAM, cloud-platform architecture, carrier commercial management, procurement, legal/compliance, privacy, security governance, and production execution authority constrain the lane.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: tenant-specific carrier SLA and interconnection agreement terms
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Public standards can define service concepts, but circuit SLAs, provider responsibilities, credits, escalation paths, and contract language are tenant-specific. The pack requires escalation when those facts change the recommendation.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: vendor and cloud provider feature behavior after review date
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Cisco, Fortinet, AWS, and Azure WAN features change frequently. The pack uses 60- to 90-day refresh windows for provider and vendor sources and requires source refresh before product-version-specific claims.

Tenant classification, privacy, retention, legal-hold, and access-approval rules govern packet captures, configs, logs, certificates, and keys. The pack blocks sensitive evidence review until those facts are supplied.

notes: Research constrains the refreshed package to WAN/SD-WAN service records, routing/tunnel evidence, provider-native cloud WAN systems, and explicit intake authority boundaries instead of generic networking advice.

No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for validated spec-pack status because tenant-specific contract, version, and sensitive-evidence questions are converted into runtime prerequisite and escalation rules.
The current package baseline is used only as legacy context and is superseded by this refreshed spec-first package.
